Reviewed via Read/Grep/Glob only.

Implementation (descriptor-only, no-write, no-real-data): service.js, policies.js, and states.js produce frozen descriptors with permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/writes_product_state/runtime_execution/runtime_lock_acquired all false; the policy fails closed (missing_context, cross_tenant_release_access -> denied_descriptor) and routes risky claims (unverified_release, missing_observability, compliance_evidence_gap, unsafe_deploy, customer_plan_mismatch) to review_required while preserving blocked claims. Synthetic identifiers only (tenant-synthetic-alpha/beta, matter-synthetic-release-readiness); no secrets/credentials/PII. registry.js section rows carry the full no-write attestation; CP878 section rows resolve to M04=10, M05=22, M06=8 = 40 with deliverable distribution implementation 16 / claude_review 2 / ui 4 / failure_recovery 4 / test 8 / contract 2 / security_audit 4, matching the pack baseline, the live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json packs[0]), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and COMMERCIAL_CP878_REQUIREMENTS.

Contract/validation: contracts/commercial-readiness-contract.json latest_pack/current_pack=CP00-878, latest_projection=CommercialCp878PermissionAuditFixtureDescriptor, top-level validation.valid=true with full no_write_attestation; compatibility alias contracts/commercial-contract.json points to the canonical contract with latest_pack_id CP00-878. validate-rp29-commercial-readiness-contract.mjs enforces the CP873->CP878 binding chain, coverage, descriptor, and contract validation. command-evidence.json shows the initial rp29:commercial:validate and node --test both exit 0; the remaining deterministic ladder (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/test/validate/spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/rp00 + git diff --check) is, per pack notes, expected to run after review normalization/adjudication/queue regeneration and before commit.

Tests are substantive and CP878-specific (model.test.js and service.test.js): assert coverage equality, descriptor validation with empty error arrays, validation-error mapping, approval/review routing without permission/audit writes, cross-tenant and missing-Matter-trace denial, and synthetic-fixture no-real-data/no-receipt invariants.

Authority boundary holds: claude_packet.read_only=true and promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; descriptor authority_boundary has claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. Hermes packet is evidence-only metadata (emits_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. README accurately states no CI/CD trigger, deployment, report generation, permission/audit writes, runtime receipts, credentials, or real client data, with H29/C29 read-only until a later runtime-opening pack with human approval.

Review-evidence integrity: readiness-report.json auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON result, non-empty), validation-report.json shows forbidden_tools_present=false and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[] (no hidden implementation). Governance artifacts (manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, claude-review-result) honestly report status in_progress / production_ready false / pending_review and do not prematurely assert production_ready or enterprise trust.
